title: Cardiac Electrophysiology (Monodomain)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Cardiac Electrophysiology (Monodomain): Cardiac EP inversion: recover 3D transmembrane potential distribution from body surface ECG recordings. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.pde.monodomain_bidomain…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~2000; torso_conductivity_uncertainty, electrode_placement_error_mm set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: J_e = sigma_e * nabla phi_e; phi_body_surface = T * phi_e; T is body-heart transfer matrix
E.world_state_x: 3D transmembrane potential V_m(x,y,z,t) across cardiac muscle
E.observation_y: body surface potentials phi_BSP(t) at N_electrodes ECG/ECGI electrodes

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D_transmembrane_potential is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 2000); torso_conductivity_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Observation gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: activation time error < 5 ms for well-constrained geometry. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~2000. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |torso_conductivity_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_mesh_nodes ** 2) for transfer matrix computation per iteration
